etymology.description: 人民(人民) (rénmín, "people"; "citizens") + 日報(日報) (rìbào, "daily newspaper") = People’s Daily, a newspaper meant for the general public
etymology.interpretation: 人民(人民) (rénmín, "people"; "citizens") combines 人(人) (rén, "person") to emphasize individual members of society and 民(民) (mín, "people") referring to the masses, creating the collective idea of a populace or nation. 日報(日報) (rìbào, "daily newspaper") combines 日(日) (rì, "day") indicating a daily occurrence and 報(報) (bào, "report"; "newspaper") to signify a daily publication of news and reports.

examples.chinese: 我每天都读人民日报(我每天都讀人民日報)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ měitiān dōu dú Rénmín Rìbào.
examples.english: I read the People’s Daily every day.

examples.chinese: 人民日报报道了这条重要新闻(人民日報報導了這條重要新聞)。
examples.pinyin: Rénmín Rìbào bàodào le zhè tiáo zhòngyào xīnwén.
examples.english: The People’s Daily reported this important news.

examples.chinese: 他在人民日报工作已有十年(他在人民日報工作已有十年)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài Rénmín Rìbào gōngzuò yǐ yǒu shí nián.
examples.english: He has been working at the People’s Daily for ten years.
